content: White Hat. Stick to facts and figures. What do you actually know, and what's still missing?
Red Hat. What do your feelings, hunches, and intuitions say? You don't have to justify them.
Black Hat. What could go wrong? Where are the risks, flaws, and weak points?
Yellow Hat. What are the benefits? Why might this actually work? Make the best case.
Green Hat. Generate alternatives. What's a new idea — however unlikely — you haven't considered?
Blue Hat. Step back. What are you really trying to achieve, and what thinking step comes next?
PMI. List the Pluses, the Minuses, and the Interesting points. Don't skip Interesting.
CAF — Consider All Factors. What have you left out? What haven't you looked at yet?
C&S — Consequences and Sequel. What follows in the short, medium, and long term?
AGO — Aims, Goals, Objectives. What are you actually trying to do here? Be specific.
FIP — First Important Priorities. Of all the factors in play, which three matter most?
APC — Alternatives, Possibilities, Choices. Force yourself to list three more options.
OPV — Other People's Views. Who is affected? What does this look like through their eyes?
Challenge an assumption you're making. Why must it be that way? What if it weren't?
Escape. Name a dominant idea in your thinking, then deliberately move away from it.
Movement, not judgement. Don't ask 'is this right?' — ask 'where does this idea take me?'
State your problem in one sentence. Now state it again in a completely different way.
Strip the thing to its function. What is it for? Could another form serve the same function?
A provocation (PO) is an idea that doesn't have to be true, only useful. Invent one now.
Reverse the usual. State the opposite of what normally happens. Where does that lead?
Exaggerate one feature to an absurd extreme. What does that reveal?
Distort. Change one fixed part of the situation. What breaks, and what becomes possible?
Separate 'ego' from 'argument'. Would you still hold this view if it wasn't yours?
Put on the {hat} Hat. {task}

task: Apply it to what you're thinking about right now.
Spend two minutes there before switching.
Write down three things from inside this hat.

content: Random word: {word}. Force a link between it and what you're working on.
Provocation (PO): {provocation}. Use it as a stepping stone — where does it lead?

provocation: you have no time at all
you must charge ten times more
you must give it away free
the user is your best friend
the least important part is the most important
you can only do one thing
nobody will ever see the result
the whole world will see the result

content: OPV: how would {person} see this? What would they notice that you don't?

person: an expert from an unrelated field
someone from a hundred years ago
someone from a hundred years in the future
a journalist looking for a story
the person who has to live with the result

content: Apply the {tool} tool to what you're working on.
